Title: Broken lifestyle
ChapterText:
People with social anxiety have usually very distorted self-image. They perceive themselves based on negative experiences from the past, situations they are mostly not responsible for.
These false image can negatively affect confidence, mental well-being and can lead to social anxiety.

To break the mental pattern and create new empowering self-image visualisation can be used.

Title: Visualisation
ChapterText:
Visualization (or Mental Rehearsal) is the practice of creating a detailed mental image of a future event.By repeatedly seeing yourself succeed, you desensitize your nervous system to the triggers that usually cause anxiety. With a little practice visualization can become natural and rewarding for you.

Now let's make a small excercise..

Title: Relaxation
ChapterText:
Now, sit or lie down in a quiet place where you wouldn't be disturbed.
Close your eyes and concentrate on your breathing. Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4, and exhale slowly for 4.

Repeat this 3 times until your heartbeat slows and body feels heavy...

Title: Mental imagery
ChapterText:
Great, now, when you feel more relaxed and grounded imagine yourself internally.
Imagine yourself in situatíon you don't usually feel comfortably in. Imagine how you easily handle the situation, how you stand with confidence and feel free.
Go to the minor details, get yourself immersed in the situation.
Feel the emotions...

Congratulations!
You successfuly visualized. Don't worry if you had troubles with the technique. No one is born master. Soon it will be easier for you to visualize.

Title: When and how much practice
ChapterText:
Ideally you should practice visualization twice a day, once before falling asleep and once right after waking up, because your subconscious mind is accessible and more susceptible.

Remember that a key to successful visualization is to imagine yourself as the main actor and feel the emotions.
